text: A challenger <sup foot_note=1229>1229</sup> has demanded a punishment bound to come

foot_notes.1229: According to 8:32, An-NaŠłćr ibn Al-Šł§√Ęrith challenged Allah, saying, ‚ÄúIf this is indeed the truth from You, then rain down stones upon us from the sky or overcome us with a painful punishment.‚ÄĚ

text: for the disbelievers‚ÄĒto be averted by none‚ÄĒ
from Allah, Lord of pathways of ňĻheavenlyňļ ascent,
ňĻthrough whichňļ the angels and the ňĻholyňļ spirit <sup foot_note=1230>1230</sup> will ascend to Him on a Day fifty thousand years in length. <sup foot_note=1231>1231</sup>

foot_notes.1230: The holy spirit is the angel Gabriel.
foot_notes.1231: Judgment Day will seem like 50 000 years for a disbeliever, but it will seem like a very short period for a believer. The Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth collected by Im√Ęm AŠł•med to have said that, for the believer, this long period will be like the time they took to perform a single prayer in the world.

text: So endure ňĻthis denial, O Prophet,ňļ with beautiful patience.
They truly see this ňĻDayňļ as impossible,
but We see it as inevitable.
On that Day the sky will be like molten brass
and the mountains like ňĻtufts ofňļ wool.
And no close friend will ask ňĻaboutňļ their friends,
ňĻalthoughňļ they will be made to see each other. The wicked will wish to ransom themselves from the punishment of that Day by their children,
their spouses, their siblings,
their clan that sheltered them,
and everyone on earth altogether, just to save themselves.
But no! There will certainly be a raging Flame
ripping off scalps.
It will summon whoever turned their backs ňĻon Allahňļ and turned away ňĻfrom the truthňļ,
and gathered and hoarded ňĻwealthňļ.
Indeed, humankind was created impatient:
distressed when touched with evil,
and withholding when touched with good‚ÄĒ
except those who pray,
consistently performing their prayers;
and who give the rightful share of their wealth
to the beggar and the poor;
and who ňĻfirmlyňļ believe in the Day of Judgment;
and those who fear the punishment of their Lord‚ÄĒ
ňĻknowing thatňļ none should feel secure from their Lord‚Äôs punishment‚ÄĒ
and those who guard their chastity <sup foot_note=1232>1232</sup>
except with their wives or those ňĻbondwomenňļ in their possession, <sup foot_note=1233>1233</sup> for then they are free from blame,
but whoever seeks beyond that are the transgressors.
ňĻThe faithful areňļ also those who are true to their trusts and covenants;
and who are honest in their testimony;
and who are ňĻproperlyňļ observant of their prayers.
These will be in Gardens, held in honour.
So what is the matter with the disbelievers that they rush ňĻhead-longňļ towards you ňĻO Prophetňļ,
from the right and the left, in groups ňĻto mock youňļ? <sup foot_note=1234>1234</sup>

foot_notes.1234: Some Meccan pagans used to gather around the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) to mock him and the believers, saying that if there is in fact a Hereafter, they are better entitled to Paradise than the poor believers.

text: Does every one of them expect to be admitted into a Garden of Bliss?
But no! Indeed, they ňĻalreadyňļ know what We created them from. <sup foot_note=1235>1235</sup>

foot_notes.1235: i.e., from a humble fluid. See 32:8.

text: So, I do swear by the Lord of ňĻallňļ the points of sunrise and sunset <sup foot_note=1236>1236</sup> that We are truly capable

foot_notes.1236: The daily points of sunrise and sunset, caused by the earth‚Äôs rotation around its own axis.

text: of replacing them with ňĻothersňļ better than them, and We cannot be prevented ňĻfrom doing soňļ.
So let them indulge ňĻin falsehoodňļ and amuse ňĻthemselvesňļ until they face their Day, which they have been threatened with‚ÄĒ
the Day they will come forth from the graves swiftly, as if racing to an idol ňĻfor a blessingňļ, <sup foot_note=1237>1237</sup>

foot_notes.1237: Or ‚Äú‚Ä¶ as if rushing towards a target.‚ÄĚ

text: with eyes downcast, utterly covered with disgrace. That is the Day they have ňĻalwaysňļ been warned of.
